title: Understanding the Forces
text: To find the ratio of the centripetal force to the child's weight, you need to calculate both forces separately. The centripetal force is given by the formula: $$F_c=\frac{m v^2}{r}$$, where $$m$$ is the mass, v is the velocity, and $$r$$ is the radius. The weight is given by $$W=m g$$, where g is the acceleration due to gravity.

title: Calculate the Centripetal Force
text: First, convert the rotational speed from revolutions per minute to meters per second. Use the formula: v=2*pi*r*(revolutions per second).

title: Calculate the Weight
text: The weight of the child can be calculated using the formula: $$W=m g$$, where $$m$$ is the mass of the child and g is the acceleration due to gravity (approximately $$9.8$$ m/s²).

title: Find the Ratio
text: Now, find the ratio of the centripetal force to the weight using the formula: $$Ratio=\frac{F_c}{W}$$.
